item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I stopped lurking to upvote this. Keep it up dude

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amazing job it is a pretty simple idea, done in a verry good way you should be proud of yourself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. I am honored to hear that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: XD you know I'm now trying to decide whether something like a laptop or typewriter or anything of the sort would allow communication… Fun thought experiment there…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of my favourite SCPs that I've read, great concept!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because I guess stabbing a laptop was just too hard for Dr. Perrin to think of.

item.title: Re: Opinion and suggestions
item.content:encoded:
Thank you. As stated I am aware of my trouble with dialog. I will look over your suggestions and implement them if I decide they work better. Thank you for your offering regardless of if I use them or not.

Edit: Yep definitely using them. Thank you for the suggestions and assistance. Although I am going to leave out the it instead of he for the d-class as I personally feel that it makes it a bit more confusing in that one instance making it sound like possibly the SCP did it instead of the D-class.

item.title: Opinion and suggestions
item.content:encoded:
So first of all, I really liked this read. Maybe because I'm new, but I think it's pretty original! It's short and simple to understand, and the tone is kept more or less the same throughout the whole piece.

However, as did a lot of people say already, the dialogue is pretty meh. I would like to propose some changes regarding sentence structure, seen that it's already been talked about in this discussion.

Test B

-Suggestion 1
Fold suggestion 1

D-90353 was sent into the testing chamber and upon spotting D-90353, SCP-5046-1 headed towards him

I had to reread the sentence at least two times because of the wierd wording here. Repeating D-90353's name just makes it look like there are two of them, or that there you meant someone else. Instead, maybe try:

Upon spotting D-90353 entering the testing chamber, SCP-5046-1 headed towards him

That way, the order of events is clearer and the sentence looks more polished :D

-Suggestion 2
Fold suggestion 2

(…)D-90353 was instructed to remove SCP-5046 and did so.

This one was easy to understand, but unpleasant to look at. It's ending just seems "anti-climatic", like you used a lot of long words just to end it with "and did so". I think it would look better if it was:

(…)D-90353 was instructed to remove SCP-5046, which it did.

Note that I used "it", to reinforce that the foundation tends to dehumanize D-class personnel.

Test E

-Suggestion 3
Fold suggestion 3

SCP-5046-1: Yes. I was created to be… Well a companion. I'm naturally a pretty friendly person. I exist primarily to be well… a pet I guess.

Here, I see that you used ellipsis to make it look like thinking between sentences. But in the second half, the thinking doesn't look natural. I would suggest that you moved the second "well" to after the elipsis, but because you already used it pretty early in the sentence, I think just removing it entirely would look better. Also the first well starts with caps, (which I think is a gramatical error) and I reckon it would make it even more natural if there was a comma after it:

SCP-5046-1: Yes. I was created to be… well, a companion. I'm naturally a pretty friendly person. I exist primarily to be… a pet I guess.

-Suggestion 4
Fold suggestion 4

Dr. Perrin: I see… Can you tell me who created you and how

It's missing a question mark :p

-Suggestion 5
Fold suggestion 5

SCP-5046-1: Well my creator was- No… Sorry you want to talk to me you're going to have to use something other than a corpse.

This sentence is too long, and with no commas it looks like SCP-5046-1 was talking really fast or that there was something behind him that was forcing him to keep talking or he would explode if he kept talking why is this sentence still going on and how do I stop.
I believe there are a few missing words to connect the sentence, it doesn't look finished (with all due respect).

My suggestion is the following:

SCP-5046-1: Well my creator was- No… Sorry, but if you want to talk to me you're going to have to use something other than a corpse.

There are a lot of ways to improve it, but I reckon this is the one that makes the less amount of tweaking and the most improvements without changing the sentence's meaning.

That's it! Sorry if it's too long, but I think the small changes can make a world of diference on the piece as a whole. I don't know if you're going to rewrite it or just edit the page, but whatever you do, I know I'm gonna like it ;)

BONUS
hide the bonus hehe

Can you tell I just learned how to make collapsibles?

item.title: small error
item.content:encoded:
in test e interview log you refered to test g. That should be changed to test F.

Closing Statement: SCP-5046-1 became unresponsive, refusing to speak further, and eventually Dr. Perrin removed SCP-5046. Despite not learning what we wanted to learn we did learn a large amount of valuable information. Dr. Perrin has proposed a plan for Test G to the Ethics committee.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So SCP-1609 is a pretty popular scip, the main plot being that the GOC are idiots and wood chipped an animate chair and it turned into an angry storm of wood chips. Every part of it is basically a joke that the GOC agents were idiots for doing it.

And here we have foundation researchers smashing an animate chair with an axe in the first test.

The P stands for protect, they wouldn’t test to see if an entity is sapient by killing it with an axe to see if it remembers.

The irony between this and SCP-1609 was enough for a -1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There was actually an earlier test in the initial version of the draft where they did attempt to animate something else though that was before the initial corpse test actually. The test led them to believe that the animated being would not be able to speak in any human language without having a proper working, whatever you would call the biological systems that allow speech, which led to the corpse test.

The initial test was removed after feedback from various others seemed to imply that it was an unneccessary extra and hurt the pacing of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I assume this would've disrupted the story you were trying to tell, but I have to wonder why they didn't try animating something like a mannequin or a plastinated cadaver before attempting it with a living human.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dialog is something I continue to struggle with and I hope that I am getting better, but I know I have more work to do. Thank you for your comment regardless and I'll see as time passes whether I can work on the dialog perhaps.

Also… I know I'm probably an idiot and should know this already, but I'm struggling to figure out what SPaG means?

Edit: Spelling and Grammar… I am an idiot. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah I must have missed those. I'll edit them immediately. As for the ending there were a couple of mentions of it being anti-climactic, but despite many attempts to figure out how I could punch up the ending I couldn't come up with any that didn't drastically change it from it's intended theme. Do you have any ideas perhaps? I'd love to be able to give it a more heavy hitting ending while still maintaining the intended message.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not too proud to acknowledge my deficiencies. I would appreciate any corrections you deem fit to make. Thank you very much for your offered assistance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the test with the coffee table (sorry I'm not adept at wikidot) there's a couple SCP XXXX's.

Also this was enjoyable +1
However just as a note the ending did feel a bit like a anti-climax

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm neutral to this one on a narrative/character front, but the errors in clinical tone, dialogue, and occasionally sentence structure really knock it down a couple of pegs for me. Author, since people seem to tentatively like it as-is, would you mind me going through and making a few corrections for wording and the like? Nothing major. Alternatively, if you rather I do a line-by-line comb through and post that here, I can do that instead.

Edit: Done. I've erred quite heavily on the side of caution, and didn't touch the dialogue; character-work is not my strongest point, and I didn't really want to "correct" the speech of another author's character.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appreciate the sudden shift in the testing logs from C to D, but the dialogue in the interviews is less than stellar and riddled with SPaG errors. I can't pretend that the characters speak how real people would.
